data.title: Frontline gives Q2 results
data.description:
• Frontline press release (NYSE:FRO): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 misses by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $480.1M (-13.7% Y/Y) beats by $151.39M.
  • Achieved average daily spot time charter equivalent earnings (“TCEs”)1 for VLCCs, Suezmax tankers and LR2/Aframax tankers in the second quarter of $43,100, $38,900 and $29,300 per day, respectively.

data.title: Germany's retail sales rise 1.9% Y/Y in July, missing estimates
data.description:
• Retail sales in Germany increased 1.90% in July over the same month in the previous year, missing consensus of 2.6%.

  • Retail sales dropped 1.50% in July over the previous month.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Trump officials say end of low-value package tariff exemption is permanent - report
data.description:
The U.S. tariff exemption for package shipments valued under $800 ends permanently on Friday, with a six-month transition period under which postal service shippers can opt to pay a flat duty of $80 to $200 per package depending on the country of origin, reported Reuters.

The report, which cited Trump administration officials, said the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agency will begin collecting normal duty rates on all global parcel imports, regardless of value, after 12:01 a.m. EDT (0401 GMT) on Friday.

"President Trump's ending of the deadly de minimis loophole will save thousands of American lives by restricting the flow of narcotics and other dangerous prohibited items, and add up to $10 billion a year in tariff revenues to our Treasury," White House trade adviser Peter Navarro told reporters, according to the report.

The move comes after President Trump in July signed an executive order that expanded the suspension of duty-free de minimis treatment for low-value shipments to a global scale, after suspending the provision for shipments from China and Hong Kong in May.

 The loophole was exploited by fast fashion retailers like SHEIN, as well as Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) to ship packages duty-free from China. As a result of the exemption, the volume of de minimis shipments entering the U.S. exploded from 134M to 1.36B over a 9-year period.

The Reuters report added that based on CBP guidance, parcels would be charged $80 from countries with Trump-imposed duty rates below 16%, such as Britain and the European Union, $160 from countries between 16% and 25%, such as Indonesia and Vietnam, and $200 from countries above 25%, including China, Brazil, India, and Canada.

data.title: Asian indexes turn lower despite record close for Wall Street
data.description:
Asian indexes traded mostly lower on Friday, as investors awaited a key U.S. inflation report and after gains in tech stocks on Wall Street helped propel the market to another all-time high.

Gold hovered around $3,410 per ounce on Friday, near its highest level in over a month, and is on track for a second consecutive weekly gain

Japan (NKY:IND) dropped 0.34% , while Japan unemployment rate dropped to 2.3% in July, retail sales rose. Japan’s housing starts shrank. The Japanese yen held around 147 per dollar on Friday, remaining sideways for nearly four weeks as investors digested a series of economic reports

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.33%, extending the previous session’s advance amid broad market strength. Chinese equities on track for monthly gains on easing US-China trade tensions, expectations of new stimulus, and stronger fund inflows, rising retail participation, and margin buying. 

The offshore yuan weakened to around 7.12 per dollar on Friday, retreating from a more than nine-month high reached in the prior session and snapping a five-day winning streak. 

China has extended anti-dumping duties on phenol imports from the U.S., the EU, South Korea, Japan, and Thailand for an additional five years, effective August 29, the Ministry of Commerce announced in a statement on Thursday.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.51%, rising for the first session in four after Wall Street’s S&P 500 and Dow Jones closed at record highs 

South Korea unveiled a record KRW 728 trillion budget plan for 2026.

Singapore’s bank loans rose to SGD 854.0 billion in July.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.15%, erasing losses from the previous two sessions. Gains were primarily driven by banking and financial services stocks. The BSE Sensex tracked an upbeat session on Wall Street overnight.

The Indian economy remains resilient, supported by strong rural demand, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) said in its monthly bulletin released on Thursday.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.14%, pulling back gains from the previous two-session rally, and on track for its first weekly loss in four weeks. Australia’s private sector credit rose 0.7% M/M in July.

The Australian dollar edged higher to around $0.654 on Friday, marking its fourth consecutive session of gains to hit a two-week high.

In the U.S. on Thursday, Wall Street’s major averages  finished higher, as investors weighed a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, all as the S&P 500 surpassed 6,500 and marked yet another record close. 

U.S. stock futures turned negative on Friday, Dow -0.17%; S&P 500 -0.05%; Nasdaq -0.15%.

 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Japan unemployment rate drops to 2.3% in July, retail sales rise
data.description:
• Japan’s unemployment rate dropped to 2.3% in July 2025, below market expectations of 2.5% and down from the same level in the previous four months.

  • It was the lowest jobless rate since December 2019, as the number of unemployed fell to 1.64 million in July, the lowest since January 2020.

  • Retail sales in Japan rose by 0.3% Y/Y in July 2025, slowing sharply from a downwardly revised 1.9% gain in the previous month and falling well short of market expectations for a 1.8% increase.

data.title: Capital One to cut 215 jobs at former Discover offices - report
data.description:
Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) plans to lay off 215 employees based at the former offices of Discover Financial Services, the Washington Business Journal reported.

According to the report, citing Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification with the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, the notice states that 193 positions will be eliminated by Oct. 17, while the remaining 22 are scheduled for termination in May 2026.

Capital One did not immediately respond to a request for comments.

The report further said the layoffs are tied to Capital One's decision to wind down Discover's mortgage business.

data.title: Gamehaus Holdings announces $5 million share repurchase program
data.description:
• Gamehaus Holdings (NASDAQ:GMHS) announced a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $5 million of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • The program is effective immediately for a one-year period through August 28, 2026.
  • The company expects to fund repurchases under this program with its existing cash balance and cash generated from operations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition receives continued listing compliance notification from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Oak Woods Acquisition (NASDAQ:OAKU) said it has received a written notice from Nasdaq, which indicated that, as a result of the company’s delay in filing its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025, the company was not in compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • The notice states that the company has until October 21, 2025, 60 calendar days from the date of the notification letter, to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OP Bancorp announces new stock repurchase program
data.description:
• OP Bancorp (NASDAQ:OPBK) on Friday announced a new stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase up to 700,000 shares of its common stock.
  • Source: Press Release
